content.problemStatement:
An integer XXX is cool if it has a primitive root modulo XXX.

The coolness of a non-empty set of NNN distinct integers S={x1,x2,…,xN}S = \{x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_N\}S={x1​,x2​,…,xN​} is the number of cool divisors of ∏i=1Nxi\prod_{i=1}^N x_i∏i=1N​xi​.

You are given four integers LLL, RRR, AAA and BBB. Let SSS be the set containing all integers between LLL and RRR (inclusive). Consider all non-empty subsets of SSS with size between AAA and BBB (inclusive). Find the sum of values of coolness of all these subsets. Since this sum can be huge, compute it modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains four space-separated integers LLL, RRR, AAA and BBB.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the total coolness modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
